scores.correctness_notes: Core API usage is accurate: juju.run(unit, action, params), Task attributes (success, results, stdout, stderr, return_code, status), error types (TaskError, TimeoutError, ValueError) all match the gold standard.
scores.hallucination_notes: task.raise_on_failure() is not listed in the gold standard Task attributes or error handling section — appears to be a fabricated method. The gold standard states juju.run() itself raises TaskError automatically on failure.
